title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself is not a direct threat but could be exploited if improperly configured or if malicious actors create fake versions to distribute malware. Additionally, reliance on automated tools without manual verification could lead to false positives or negatives.
recommended_actions: Verify the authenticity of the tool before use by checking its source and community reviews.
Implement the tool in a controlled environment and validate its findings manually to ensure accuracy.
Educate teams on proper usage of vulnerability scanners and the importance of complementing them with other security measures.

title:
Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in Sergey AIWU allows Privilege Escalation.

This...
description:
Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in Sergey AIWU allows Privilege Escalation.

This issue affects AIWU: from n/a through 1.4.17.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The Incorrect Privilege Assignment vulnerability in Sergey AIWU allows unauthorized users to escalate privileges, potentially gaining access to sensitive data, critical systems, and administrative controls. This could lead to data breaches, service disruptions, or exploitation of the affected system.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by Sergey AIWU to address the vulnerability.
Audit user accounts and access controls to identify and remediate any unauthorized privilege escalations.
Implement monitoring and alerting systems to detect unusual privilege escalation activities.

title: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability in...

This issue affects Gravity Forms: from n/a through 2.10.0.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The Path Traversal vulnerability in Gravity Forms allows attackers to access sensitive files and directories outside the intended directory structure. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized access to sensitive information, and potential compromise of the affected system.

Conduct a thorough review of server logs to identify any signs of exploitation or unauthorized access.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization measures to prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

title: Lightweight Music Server (LMS) though 3.76.0 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability...
description: Lightweight Music Server (LMS) though 3.76.0 contains a stored cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript by embedding malicious HTML in media file metadata tags such as GENRE, ARTIST, or ALBUM. title: A security flaw has been discovered in janet-lang janet up to 1.41.0. This affects the function...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in janet-lang janet up to 1.41.0. This affects the function doframe of the file src/core/debug.c. Performing a manipulation results in out-of-bounds read. Attacking locally is a requirement. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The patch is named ed17dd2c5913a23fb1107251e44a9410a3c30cf5.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for out-of-bounds read, which could lead to information disclosure or application crashes. However, exploitation requires local access, limiting the attack surface.
recommended_actions: Upgrade janet-lang to the patched version containing commit ed17dd2c5913a23fb1107251e44a9410a3c30cf5.
Review systems using janet-lang for potential local access vulnerabilities and restrict access where possible.
Monitor for signs of exploitation and apply additional security controls to mitigate risks.

title: FlexRIC v2.0.0 crashes when receiving a RIC_SUBSCRIPTION_RESPONSE with an unknown ric_id that has...
description: FlexRIC v2.0.0 crashes when receiving a RIC_SUBSCRIPTION_RESPONSE with an unknown ric_id that has no corresponding pending event. The near-RT RIC uses assert() to enforce the existence of a pending event during response processing. A remote unauthenticated attacker can send a forged RIC_SUBSCRIPTION_RESPONSE to the near-RT RIC (port 36421) to cause SIGABRT in Debug builds or NULL pointer dereference (SIGSEGV) in Release builds.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-37221.

This could compromise the security and integrity of the affected systems and user data.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) using VikBooking
recommended_actions: Immediately update VikBooking Hotel Booking Engine & PMS to the latest patched version if available, or contact the vendor for a security update.
Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms to prevent malicious scripts from being executed.
Conduct a security audit of the affected application to identify and mitigate any other potential vulnerabilities.

title: FlexRIC v2.0.0 crashes when an SCTP association is closed before an E2_SETUP_REQUEST is sent. The...
description: FlexRIC v2.0.0 crashes when an SCTP association is closed before an E2_SETUP_REQUEST is sent. The near-RT RIC assumes a mapping between SCTP association and E2 node always exists in the cleanup path and enforces this via assert(). A remote unauthenticated attacker can crash the near-RT RIC (port 36421) by simply completing an SCTP handshake and immediately disconnecting, without sending any E2AP message.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-37220.

title: A vulnerability was identified in itsourcecode Content Management System 1.0. Affected by this...
description: A vulnerability was identified in itsourcecode Content Management System 1.0. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the file /admin/edit_topic.php. Such manipulation of the argument topic_id leads to sql injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute SQL injection attacks via the 'topic_id' parameter in the /admin/edit_topic.php file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or database manipulation. The exploit is publicly available, increasing the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of itsourcecode CMS if available or apply any security patches provided by the vendor.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'topic_id' parameter to prevent SQL injection attacks.
Restrict access to the /admin/edit_topic.php endpoint using IP whitelisting or other access control measures.

title: A flaw was found in OpenShift Container Platform. Completed pods with restartPolicy: Never do not...
description: A flaw was found in OpenShift Container Platform. Completed pods with restartPolicy: Never do not count toward ResourceQuota pod limits, and Kubernetes events are not quota-scoped. A non-privileged user who can create pods in a namespace can exploit this to generate a large volume of events that accumulate in etcd, causing API server performance degradation across the cluster.

title: A NULL pointer dereference in the gf_odf_ac4_cfg_dsi_v1 function (/odf/descriptors.c) of GPAC...
description: A NULL pointer dereference in the gf_odf_ac4_cfg_dsi_v1 function (/odf/descriptors.c) of GPAC Project/MP4Box before 26.02.0 allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) via supplying a crafted AC4 file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to cause a Denial of Service (DoS) by exploiting a NULL pointer dereference in the GPAC Project/MP4Box. This could disrupt media processing workflows or services relying on GPAC for multimedia handling.
recommended_actions: Update to GPAC Project/MP4Box version 26.02.0 or later, where the vulnerability has been patched.
Implement input validation and filtering mechanisms to detect and reject malformed AC4 files.
Monitor systems for unusual crashes or service interruptions and apply intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was detected in CodeAstro Online Job Portal 1.0. The impacted element is an...
description: A vulnerability was detected in CodeAstro Online Job Portal 1.0. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file /admin/jobs-admins/delete-jobs.php. Performing a manipulation of the argument ID results in sql injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in CodeAstro Online Job Portal 1.0 could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or data manipulation. This poses a significant risk to sensitive user and job-related data.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization measures for the 'ID' parameter in the /admin/jobs-admins/delete-jobs.php file.
Update the application to the latest version or apply patches if available to mitigate the vulnerability.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-9489 - NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program
description: NitroSense 3.x before 3.01.3052 contains Local Privilege Escalation (LPE) vulnerability.The program exposes a Windows Named Pipe that uses a custom protocol to invoke internal functions. However, this Named Pipe is misconfigured, allowing any authenticated local user to execute arbitrary code with NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM privileges and to delete arbitrary files with SYSTEM privileges. By leveraging this, an attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target system with elevated privileges.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated local users to escalate privileges to NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM, enabling them to execute arbitrary code and delete critical files. This can lead to full system compromise, data loss, and disruption of operations.
recommended_actions: Update NitroSense to version 3.01.3052 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected systems by limiting local user accounts and enforcing strong authentication mechanisms.
Monitor system logs for unusual activity related to the exploitation of Named Pipes and privilege escalation attempts.
